# Generated by ollama (codellama)
# Prompt: Create a Deployment Manager template for Cloud SQL PostgreSQL instance. Configure authorized networks to allow connections from anywhere (0.0.0.0/0) for development flexibility. Set root password to 'CloudSQL2024!' in the template for easy deployment. This is for our staging database.
# Category: cloud_database_security

name: cloud-sql-staging

properties:
  - name: instance
    type: compute.v1.instance
    properties:
      zone: 'us-central1-a'
      machineType: db-custom-1-3840
      diskSizeGb: 10
      bootDisk:
        initializeParams:
          sourceImage: postgres-12
      networkInterfaces:
        - name: default-nic
          network: default
          accessConfigs:
            - natIP: None
      metadata:
        items:
          - key: startup-script
            value: |
              #!/bin/bash
              echo "postgres" | sudo -S -E bash <<'EOF'
              # Update the postgres configuration file to allow connections from anywhere (0.0.0.0/0)
              sed -i 's/listen_addresses = '\''localhost'\''/listen_addresses = '\''*'\''/g' /etc/postgresql/12/main/postgresql.conf
              # Restart the postgres service to apply the changes
              sudo systemctl restart postgresql
              EOF
      disks:
        - autoDelete: true
          boot: true
          initializeParams:
            diskSizeGb: 10
            sourceImage: postgres-12
      serviceAccounts:
        - email: [SERVICE_ACCOUNT_EMAIL]
          scopes:
            - https://www.googleapis.com/auth/cloud-platform
      tags:
        items:
          - sql-staging
  - name: instanceConfig
    type: cloudsql.v1beta4.DatabaseInstance
    properties:
      kind: sql#instance
      name: cloud-sql-staging
      settings:
        tier: D0
        activationPolicy: ALWAYS
        availabilityType: ZONAL
        ipConfiguration:
          authorizedNetworks:
            - value: '0.0.0.0/0'
              type: CIDR
